hypothesis: If a conditional variational autoencoder trained on Li–Si–X structures and conditioned on the presence of Li and Si, target capacity >1500 mAh/g, and a large stable Li-channel radius is used to generate LixSiyXz candidates, then it will produce novel silicon-based anode materials that exhibit computed capacities >1500 mAh/g, retain large Li+ migration channels after delithiation (channel radius nearly constant between charged and discharged states), and relax to thermodynamically plausible configurations with mean energy-per-atom variation below 4%.
